conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 11 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Treatment Class
Definition: What is the categorization or classification of the treatment or therapy received by participants in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="androgen-receptor inhibitor (Darolutamide) in combination therapy" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Darolutamide is described as a 'potent androgen-receptor inhibitor' and the overall therapy is a 'combination of darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel'."
  B: value="Androgen-receptor inhibitor" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states: 'Darolutamide is a potent androgen-receptor inhibitor'."

---
Column 2: Trial
Definition: What name of the clinical study or experiment being referenced?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="ARASENS" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The trial is named 'ARASENS' in the abstract and introduction."
  B: value="ARASENS" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 mentions 'ARASENS Trial Investigators' and 'ARASENS ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02799602'."

---
Column 3: Type of Therapy
Definition: What is the description of the type of treatment or intervention provided to participants in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT), and docetaxel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The abstract states: 'a combination of darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel'."
  B: value="Combination of darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel (treatment arm); Placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel (control arm)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 describes the interventions for both arms: 'darolutamide (at a dose of 600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily) or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'."

---
Column 4: Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treatment
Definition: What percentage of participants in the treatment arm of the clinical trial who received docetaxel before or during the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="571 of 652 (87.6%)" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The results section states: 'Six cycles of docetaxel were completed in 571 of 652 patients (87.6%) in the darolutamide group'."
  B: value="571 (87.6%)" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states: 'Six cycles of docetaxel were completed in 571 of 652 patients (87.6%) in the darolutamide group'."

---
Column 5: Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Control
Definition: What number and percentage of participants who received docetaxel before or during the trial in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="556 of 650 (85.5%)" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The results section states: 'and in 556 of 650 patients (85.5%) in the placebo group'."
  B: value="556 (85.5%)" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states: 'Six cycles of docetaxel were completed... in 556 of 650 patients (85.5%) in the placebo group.'"

---
Column 6: Median Age (years) | Treatment
Definition: What median age in years of participants included in the treatment arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="67" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="Table 1, 'Median age (range) — yr' row, 'Darolutamide-ADT-Docetaxel (N=651)' column shows '67 (41-89)'."
  B: value="67" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="Page 5, Table 1, 'Median age (range) — yr' for 'Darolutamide—ADT—Docetaxel (N=651)†' is '67 (41–89)'."

---
Column 7: Median Age (years) | Control
Definition: What the median age of participants included in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="67" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="Table 1, 'Median age (range) — yr' row, 'Placebo-ADT-Docetaxel (N=654)' column shows '67 (42-86)'."
  B: value="67" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="Page 5, Table 1, 'Median age (range) — yr' for 'Placebo—ADT—Docetaxel (N=654)†' is '67 (42–86)'."

---
Column 8: Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Treatment
Definition: What is the median duration in months that participants in the treatment arm of the trial received on-treatment therapy? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="41.0" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The results section states: 'the median treatment duration was longer in the darolutamide group (41.0 months)'."
  B: value="41.0" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states: 'the median treatment duration was longer in the darolutamide group (41.0 months)'."

---
Column 9: Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Control
Definition: What is the median duration in months of participants receiving treatment in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="16.7" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The results section states: 'than in the placebo group (16.7 months)'."
  B: value="16.7" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states: 'the median treatment duration was longer in the darolutamide group (41.0 months) than in the placebo group (16.7 months)'."

---
Column 10: No. of Deaths - N | Treatment
Definition: What the number of participants who died in the treatment arm of the clinical trial?.
  A: value="229" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The results section states: 'The primary analysis of overall survival was performed after 533 patients had died (229 patients in the darolutamide group and 304 patients in the placebo group)'."
  B: value="229" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states: 'The primary analysis of overall survival was performed after 533 patients had died (229 patients in the darolutamide group and 304 patients in the placebo group).'"

---
Column 11: No. of Deaths - N | Control
Definition: What number of deaths that occurred in the control arm of the clinical trial?.
  A: value="304" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The results section states: 'The primary analysis of overall survival was performed after 533 patients had died (229 patients in the darolutamide group and 304 patients in the placebo group)'."
  B: value="304" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states: 'The primary analysis of overall survival was performed after 533 patients had died (229 patients in the darolutamide group and 304 patients in the placebo group).'"

---
Column 12: PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment
Definition: What the hazard ratio with confidence intervals of progression-free survival (PFS) of all comparisons in all participants, regardless of metastasis type or volume, in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include the rate (percentage) and any accompanying details. If multiple arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document reports 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as a secondary endpoint, which is a form of PFS. Table 2 provides the median time as 'NR' and 'Patients with Event' as '225 (35%)', but not a direct percentage rate of PFS at a specific time point for the treatment arm."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="PFS Rate was not found in the provided pages or search results."

---
Column 13: PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control
Definition: What hazard ratio with confidence intervals of Progression-Free Survival (PFS) of all comparisons in all participants in the control arm of the trial? If reported, include the rate (percentage) and any accompanying details. If multiple arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document reports 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as a secondary endpoint, which is a form of PFS. Table 2 provides the median time as '19.1' months and 'Patients with Event' as '391 (60%)', but not a direct percentage rate of PFS at a specific time point for the control arm."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="PFS Rate was not found in the provided pages or search results."

---
Column 14: TTPSA (mo) | Treatment
Definition: What time To Progression of Serum Androgen (in months) in the treatment arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reached (NR)" | page=6 | modality=table | reasoning="Table 2, 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' row, 'Darolutamide-ADT-Docetaxel (N=651)' column shows 'NR' for median months. This is a common surrogate for TTPSA."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="TTPSA was not found in the provided pages or search results."

---
Column 15: TTPSA (mo) | Control
Definition: What time to Progressive Serum Androgen (TTPSA) in months for participants in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="19.1" | page=6 | modality=table | reasoning="Table 2, 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' row, 'Placebo-ADT-Docetaxel (N=654)' column shows '19.1' for median months. This is a common surrogate for TTPSA."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="TTPSA was not found in the provided pages or search results."


Submit verification for easy columns first. For disputed columns, use get_page then submit.

Figure 1. Overall Survival (Full Analysis Set).
Kaplan–Meier estimates of overall survival are shown. For the analysis of overall survival, data were censored as of the last known date
the patients were alive. One patient who was randomly assigned to the placebo group but received darolutamide was included in the
placebo group in the full analysis set. CI denotes confidence interval, and NE not estimable.



SECONDARY EFFICACY END POINTS
Darolutamide was associated with significantly
greater benefits than placebo for the first five sec-
ondary efficacy end points tested hierarchically



(Table 2 and Fig. 2). The time to development of
castration-resistant disease was significantly lon-
ger in the darolutamide group (hazard ratio, 0.36;
95% CI, 0.30 to 0.42; P<0.001). The time to pain
